title: postgres container volume not empty
body:
### Issue Description

Using the following compose file:

services:
  db:
    container_name: db
    image: postgres:latest
    restart: no
    volumes:
      - db:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"

volumes:
  db:

Cannot start the db container and it errors out saying that the /var/lib/postgresql/data

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run the compose file


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected. It should work normally. Exact same compose put on docker compose, it works. Both podman compose and docker compose are using docker compose under the hood so no clue why its not working.

comments.body: Please provide the full error from Podman, ideally including a full reproducer - meaning the command or commands which cause the issue when run.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

Sorry for the delay.
Podman itself is not giving an error. The compose practically works and the container gets spinned up. However, when i try to connect to the postgres instance, i see that the container has not been fully setup. The postgres container is giving an error saying that /var/lib/postgresql/data contains data and that I should upgrade any old instance of the db. 
I deleted the named volume multiple times and took down the compose, then brought it up again. Same issue
Does the image contain data in `/var/lib/postgresql/data`? If it does, we'd copy that information into the volume, populating it with data and potentially making Postgres angry?
It should not. Trying this same image with docker compose gave no postgres errors.

@Rashnan Please check if the `db` volume is empty by adding this startup command and changing one line:

```bash
restart: always
command: "ls -al /var/lib/postgresql/data"
```

Then check the logs:

```bash
podman logs <insert_your_container_name_here>
```

You will see the repeating output of the ´ls` command. Please be aware that Postgres needs an empty directory, so no **hidden dot-files** are allowed!

Output: ``` 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_xact -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 88 Nov 24 16:03 postgresql.auto.conf -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 30777 Nov 24 16:03 postgresql.conf -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 36 Nov 24 16:06 postmaster.opts total 48 drwx------ 1 postgres 1000 4096 Nov 24 16:06 . drwxrwxrwt 3 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:22 .. drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 base drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:06 global drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_commit_ts drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_dynshmem -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 5743 Nov 24 16:03 pg_hba.conf -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 2640 Nov 24 16:03 pg_ident.conf drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:06 pg_logical drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_multixact drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_notify drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_replslot drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_serial drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_snapshots drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:06 pg_stat drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_stat_tmp drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_subtrans drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_tblspc drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_twophase -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 3 Nov 24 16:03 PG_VERSION drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_wal drwx------ 1 postgres postgres 4096 Nov 24 16:03 pg_xact -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 88 Nov 24 16:03 postgresql.auto.conf -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 30777 Nov 24 16:03 postgresql.conf -rw------- 1 postgres postgres 36 Nov 24 16:06 postmaster.opts ``` I believe I have discovered the reason for the issue: docker had a pulled version of postgres17, while podman was pulling the actual latest postgres18. postgres18 is when this configuration issue changed. This was the reason why docker was not giving this error while podman was. After I identified this issue, I started looking into the error message itself a bit more and I realized this was a simple change in compose file syntax that postgres18 now mandates. The original error message would have been helpful to identify this so I apologize for not being able to provide that from the beginning. As you can see the issue is in how postgres18 now handles creation of the /var/lib/postgresql/data directory and postgres18 now mandates binding to /var/lib/postgresql (the parent dir) instead
Once you can replicate this on your end I would appreciate it if you could close this issue

@Rashnan There's **no issue**, but you pulled the wrong version of the Postgres container. It's likely that you pulled Postgres version 17 when using Docker some time ago. But now when running Podman it has to pull that image again. It's working as intended.

That's your current `compose.yaml`:

```
services:
  db:
    container_name: db
    image: postgres:latest
    restart: no
    volumes:
      - db:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
```

-> As you can see you pulled the container image `postgres:latest` and `latest` is **version 18**. When you want to get version 17 you need to change it to this image:

```
services:
  db:
    container_name: db
    image: postgres:17
    restart: no
    volumes:
      - db:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
```

-> You can find the different image versions [here](https://hub.docker.com/_/postgres).

Never configure `latest` when you want to stay on a specific version.

This issue can be closed.

I had not intended to stay on a specific version, I did want it to work on postgres latest version (18).
I however assumed that docker would also have the latest version of postgres (18) since it had the latest tag. Docker had postgres 17 image already (i assumed it did have an image but i assumed it to also be postgres18) so it did not try to look for a newer image, podman had no image so it pulled the latest which would be 18.

There is no issue and this is all expected behaviour and my apologies.
